title: gilia lyndana
intro: Gilia lyndana, also known as the Lindley's gilia, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gilia lyndana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. Gilia lyndana is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusion or decoction of its flowers, leaves, and stems, which can be consumed as a tea or used in herbal remedies.
